production.name: production
production.description: In HSE, the production department refers to areas and teams directly involved in manufacturing, processing, or assembling products. Observations here often relate to machine operation, line safety, process hazards, and worker behaviors during active production. Typical events include equipment malfunctions, unsafe work practices, or exposure to hazardous materials.
examples: Operator bypassed machine guard during shift.
Spill of coolant observed near the assembly line.
Worker not wearing hearing protection in production area.
Conveyor belt emergency stop was blocked.
Loose material found on production floor causing trip hazard.

maintenance.name: maintenance
maintenance.description: The maintenance department handles the upkeep, repair, and servicing of equipment and facilities. HSE observations here often involve lockout/tagout compliance, use of tools, working at heights, and exposure to electrical or mechanical hazards during repairs or preventive maintenance.
examples: Technician did not use lockout procedure before servicing pump.
Oil spill left uncleaned after maintenance work.
Missing guard on maintenance tool cart.
Worker used ladder without fall protection during light replacement.
Improper storage of chemicals in maintenance area.

quality_control.name: quality_control
quality_control.description: Quality control involves inspecting, testing, and verifying products or processes to ensure standards are met. HSE issues in this department may include handling of test samples, use of inspection equipment, ergonomic risks, and safe storage of rejected materials.
examples: Inspector not wearing gloves while handling chemical samples.
Trip hazard from cables in quality lab.
Rejected parts stored in walkway.
Sharp edges found on test equipment.
Spillage of test solution during product inspection.

logistics.name: logistics
logistics.description: The logistics department manages the movement, storage, and distribution of materials and products. HSE observations here often relate to vehicle safety, loading/unloading practices, manual handling, and traffic management within the facility.
examples: Forklift operated at excessive speed in loading bay.
Pallets stacked unsafely in shipping area.
Driver did not use seatbelt in company vehicle.
Blocked emergency exit in logistics zone.
Manual lifting of heavy boxes without assistance.

administration.name: administration
administration.description: Administration covers office-based staff and activities such as HR, finance, and management. HSE concerns here include office ergonomics, fire safety, slips/trips, and emergency preparedness in administrative areas.
examples: Blocked fire exit in admin office.
Loose electrical cords under desks.
Employee reported wrist pain from poor workstation setup.
Spilled coffee not cleaned up in hallway.
Missing evacuation map in administration area.

safety.name: safety
safety.description: The safety department is responsible for developing, implementing, and monitoring health and safety policies and procedures. Observations here may include safety audits, training sessions, incident investigations, and compliance checks.
examples: Safety officer conducted PPE compliance inspection.
Near miss reported during safety walkthrough.
Outdated safety signage found in corridor.
Incomplete incident investigation documentation.
Safety training session not attended by all staff.

engineering.name: engineering
engineering.description: Engineering involves the design, modification, and technical support of equipment, processes, and facilities. HSE issues in this department may include risk assessments, design for safety, and safe execution of technical projects or modifications.
examples: Design change did not include safety review.
Engineer entered restricted area without permit.
Temporary modification lacked proper guarding.
Risk assessment missing for new equipment installation.
Blueprints did not specify emergency shutoff locations.

warehouse.name: warehouse
warehouse.description: The warehouse department is responsible for storing raw materials, components, and finished goods. HSE observations here often involve storage practices, racking safety, forklift operations, and housekeeping to prevent slips, trips, and falls.
examples: Boxes stacked above safe height in warehouse.
Spilled oil on warehouse floor not cleaned promptly.
Damaged racking identified during inspection.
Pedestrian walkway blocked by pallets.
Forklift parked in emergency access route.

operations.name: operations
operations.description: Operations refers to the overall running of core business activities, often encompassing production, logistics, and support functions. HSE events here may include process upsets, cross-departmental hazards, and general operational risks affecting multiple teams.
examples: Process upset led to unplanned shutdown.
Operations staff failed to report minor spill.
Cross-departmental safety drill conducted.
Unclear handover between shifts caused confusion.
General housekeeping issues noted in operations area.

facility_management.name: facility_management
facility_management.description: Facility management oversees the maintenance, safety, and functionality of buildings and infrastructure. HSE observations here include building safety checks, fire system maintenance, waste management, and ensuring safe access and egress.
examples: Fire alarm system overdue for inspection.
Blocked emergency exit in facility corridor.
Leaking roof reported to facility management.
Improper storage of cleaning chemicals in janitor closet.
Lighting failure in stairwell not addressed promptly.
